attractions.name: Adams Morgan
attractions.description: Adams Morgan is a vibrant, culturally diverse neighborhood in Northwest Washington, D.C., renowned for its eclectic nightlife, independent shops, and global culinary scene. Historically a hub for counterculture and the arts, it features colorful 19th-century Victorian rowhouses and is a major center for the city's LGBTQ community.
attractions.address: 2424 18th St NW, Suite C2, Washington, DC 20009
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Individual business hours vary)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Iconic nightlife and live music venues along 18th Street NW', 'Diverse international dining options featuring Ethiopian, Salvadoran, and Vietnamese cuisines', "Adams Morgan Day, the city's longest-running multicultural street festival", 'Historic 19th-century Victorian architecture and colorful murals', "The famous 'jumbo slice' pizza, a staple of the neighborhood's late-night food scene", 'Unity Park, which hosts a seasonal Saturday farmers market']
attractions.tips: Parking is extremely limited, so visitors are encouraged to use public transit (Woodley Park or Columbia Heights Metro) or ride-sharing. Visit on a Saturday to explore the local farmers market at Unity Park, and wear comfortable walking shoes to navigate the neighborhood's historic, hilly streets.
attractions.nearby: Meridian Hill Park (Malcolm X Park), Smithsonian National Zoological Park, Dupont Circle, Rock Creek Park, Columbia Heights

attractions.name: American University
attractions.description: American University is a private federally chartered research university in Washington, D.C., known for its strong programs in international service, public policy, and global education. Established by an Act of Congress in 1893, the campus is a designated arboretum and serves as a vibrant hub for academic and cultural life in the nation's capital. American University, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Campus Grounds: Open 24/7; Admissions Welcome Center: Monday–Friday 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Select Saturdays 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM; American University Museum: Wednesday–Sunday 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM. Experience AU, AU Museum
attractions.admission: Free (Campus grounds and American University Museum) American University Museum, Visiting AU
highlights: ['The Main Quad: A central gathering spot and the heart of the historic campus.', "Katzen Arts Center: A 130,000-square-foot facility housing the university's art, music, and theatre departments.", 'American University Museum: Features rotating exhibitions of regional, national, and international contemporary art.', 'Campus Arboretum: The entire 90-acre campus is an arboretum featuring 10 distinct themed gardens.', 'School of International Service (SIS) Building: A LEED Gold-certified structure known for its sustainable design and global focus.', "Bender Library: The university's main library, home to over a million books and extensive research resources."]
attractions.tips: The closest Metrorail station is Tenleytown-AU on the Red Line; a free AU shuttle runs frequently between the station and campus. Visitor parking is available in the Katzen Arts Center garage. Visiting Campus
attractions.nearby: Washington National Cathedral, Glover Archbold Park, Tenleytown, Spring Valley Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Arena Stage at the Mead Center for American Theater
attractions.description: Arena Stage is a pioneer of the American regional theater movement and the first racially integrated theater in Washington, D.C. Located on the Southwest waterfront, it is dedicated to producing and developing innovative American plays and musicals across its three unique stages. (Wikipedia), (Arena Stage)
attractions.hours: Box Office Phone: Tuesday – Sunday, 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM; In-person Box Office: Two hours before each performance; Parking Garage: 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM. (Arena Stage), (Arena Stage)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday – Sunday (Box Office), with performances typically held almost every night of the week. (Arena Stage), (The Wharf DC)
attractions.admission: Single ticket prices typically range from $44.00 to $93.00, though prices vary by production and performance date. Group discounts and special offers like 'If You Know You Know' for free tickets to first-time visitors are also available. (Arena Stage), (Arena Stage)
highlights: 'First regional theater to receive a Tony Award for theatrical excellence. ([Wikipedia)', "Pioneer of the 'theater-in-the-round' concept with the iconic Fichandler Stage. (Washington.org)", "World premiere venue for award-winning productions like 'Dear Evan Hansen' and 'The Great White Hope'. (Wikipedia)", 'Home to the Mead Center for American Theater, a $135 million glass-enclosed architectural landmark. (Wikipedia)', "Dedicated to American playwrights through initiatives like 'Power Plays'. (Arena Stage)"]
attractions.tips: First-time visitors can call and mention 'If You Know You Know' for a chance at free tickets. It is highly recommended to take public transit as the Waterfront Metro station is just one block away. Advance parking reservations are available but limited and expensive. (Arena Stage), (Arena Stage)
attractions.nearby: The Wharf DC, Waterfront Park, Titanic Memorial, International Spy Museum, and the National Mall (short distance away). (The Wharf DC), (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Arthur M. Sackler Gallery (National Museum of Asian Art)
attractions.description: The Arthur M. Sackler Gallery, part of the Smithsonian's National Museum of Asian Art, is a premier institution dedicated to the study and exhibition of Asian art from ancient times to the present. Most of the gallery is located underground beneath the Enid A. Haupt Garden, and it is physically connected to the Freer Gallery of Art and the National Museum of African Art. (National Museum of Asian Art, Wikipedia)
attractions.city: Washington, DC (National Museum of Asian Art)
attractions.hours: Monday: 10 a.m.–5:30 p.m.; Tuesday: 10 a.m.–5:30 p.m.; Wednesday: 10 a.m.–5:30 p.m.; Thursday: 10 a.m.–5:30 p.m.; Friday: 10 a.m.–5:30 p.m.; Saturday: 10 a.m.–5:30 p.m.; Sunday: 10 a.m.–5:30 p.m. (National Museum of Asian Art)
attractions.days_open: Daily except December 25 (National Museum of Asian Art)
attractions.admission: Free (National Museum of Asian Art)
highlights: 'Renowned collection of ancient Chinese jades and bronzes ([National Museum of Asian Art)', 'Significant holdings of South and Southeast Asian sculpture (Arthur M. Sackler Foundation)', 'Diverse exhibitions of arts from the Islamic world, including ceramics and manuscripts (National Museum of Asian Art)', 'Rotating contemporary Asian art installations (National Museum of Asian Art)', 'Underground connection to the Freer Gallery of Art and the National Museum of African Art (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Admission is always free and no tickets are required for entry. The museum is largely underground, so be sure to explore levels B1 through B3. Manual wheelchairs are available to borrow at the Independence Avenue entrance. (National Museum of Asian Art, Smithsonian Social Narrative)
attractions.nearby: National Museum of African Art, Smithsonian Institution Building (The Castle), Enid A. Haupt Garden, Freer Gallery of Art (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Audi Field
attractions.description: Audi Field is a state-of-the-art soccer-specific stadium located in the Buzzard Point neighborhood of Washington, D.C. Opened in 2018, it serves as the home for D.C. United (MLS), the Washington Spirit (NWSL), and the DC Defenders (UFL), offering a 20,000-seat capacity and world-class event spaces. Wikipedia, Audi Field
attractions.hours: Box Office: Opens 4 hours prior to D.C. United matches (1.5 hours for Spirit) until halftime; Club Shop: Tue-Thu & Sat 12 PM - 5 PM, Fri 12 PM - 7 PM, Closed Sun-Mon (Matchday hours vary); Administrative/Lost & Found: Mon-Fri 9 AM - 5 PM. Audi Field A-Z Guide, Washington Spirit Matchday Guide
attractions.days_open: Open year-round for scheduled matches, private events, and tours; Club Shop and Administrative offices have specific weekday hours. Audi Field
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event; D.C. United single match tickets typically range from approximately $25 to over $200 for premium seating, while Washington Spirit matches generally start around $21 to $51 depending on the matchup and seat location. SeatGeek, Gametime
attractions.phone: (202) 600-9098 (D.C. United Tickets), (202) 536-5999 (Washington Spirit Tickets), (771) 200-3628 (DC Defenders). D.C. United, Washington Spirit, Washington.org
highlights: 'Home to D.C. United (MLS), Washington Spirit (NWSL), and DC Defenders (UFL) [Audi Field', 'Eco-friendly design with state-of-the-art amenities and 31 premium suites Audi Field', 'Located at Buzzard Point with stunning views of the city and waterfront Wikipedia', 'Features a dedicated Fan Plaza for pre-match activities Audi Field', 'Cashless venue with reverse ATMs available for guest convenience Audi Field', 'Comprehensive bicycle valet service and easy Metro access via Navy Yard station Audi Field']
attractions.tips: Audi Field is a strictly cashless venue, so bring a credit or debit card; backpacks and large bags are prohibited, so use a small clear bag for faster entry; take the Green Line to Navy Yard-Ballpark station for the easiest transit; use the complimentary bike valet if cycling to the match. Audi Field A-Z Guide, Audi Field Getting To
attractions.nearby: Nationals Park (home of the Washington Nationals), The Wharf DC (waterfront dining and shopping), Fort Lesley J. McNair, Navy Yard neighborhood. Washingtonian, The Wharf DC

attractions.name: Barracks Row (8th Street SE)
attractions.description: Barracks Row is the oldest commercial corridor in Washington, DC, serving as a historic main street that connects Capitol Hill to the Anacostia River. Named after the nearby Marine Barracks, it is a vibrant culinary and entertainment destination featuring a mix of Michelin-starred restaurants, local boutiques, and rich military history.
attractions.address: 8th Street SE (between Pennsylvania Ave SE and M Street SE)
attractions.hours: District is accessible 24/7; individual shops typically 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; restaurants and bars often open until 10:00 PM or later.
attractions.admission: Free (Public commercial corridor; individual businesses and attractions like the Marine Barracks parades may require reservations)
highlights: ['Historic Marine Barracks (8th & I Streets), the oldest post in the Corps', "Michelin-starred dining including Rose's Luxury and Pineapple & Pearls", "Barracks Row Heritage Trail ('Tour of Duty') self-guided walking tour", 'The Miracle Theatre, a restored 1909 vaudeville and silent-film house', 'Eastern Market Metro Park, a community hub for events and relaxation', 'Friday Evening Parades at the Marine Barracks (seasonal, May-August)']
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for popular restaurants like Rose's Luxury; for the Marine Barracks Friday parades, book well in advance online as they are free but require tickets. The neighborhood is very walkable and directly accessible via the Eastern Market Metro station.
attractions.nearby: Eastern Market, U.S. Capitol Building, Washington Navy Yard, National Museum of the U.S. Navy, Nationals Park

attractions.name: Basilica of the National Shrine of the Immaculate Conception (National Shrine)
attractions.description: The Basilica of the National Shrine of the Immaculate Conception is the largest Roman Catholic church in North America and ranks among the ten largest in the world. As the nation's preeminent Marian shrine, it is dedicated to the patroness of the United States and serves as a significant center for prayer, pilgrimage, and ecclesiastical art (National Shrine, Washington.org).
attractions.address: 400 Michigan Avenue, Northeast, Washington, D.C. 20017 (National Shrine)
attractions.hours: Basilica: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM daily; Gift Shop & Bookstore: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM daily; Cafeteria: Saturday – Sunday 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM (National Shrine)
attractions.admission: Free (National Shrine, Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'Home to more than 80 chapels and oratories reflecting diverse cultures ([National Shrine)', "Contains the world's largest collection of contemporary ecclesiastical art (National Shrine)", 'Features the Trinity Dome, one of the largest mosaics of its kind in the world (Wikipedia)', 'Hosts a majestic Knights (Bell) Tower (National Shrine)', 'Visited by high-profile figures including Pope Francis, Pope Benedict XVI, and Saint Mother Teresa (National Shrine)']
attractions.tips: Guided tours are available Monday through Saturday but are not offered on Sundays. Visitors can enjoy free organ concerts on Sundays during July and August, and the entire facility is wheelchair accessible (National Shrine, Washington.org).
attractions.nearby: Saint John Paul II National Shrine, Franciscan Monastery of the Holy Land, President Lincoln's Cottage, Catholic University of America (Tripadvisor, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Blair House (The President's Guest House)
attractions.description: Blair House, officially known as The President's Guest House, is a complex of four connected 19th-century row houses that serves as the primary residence for foreign heads of state visiting the President. This historic facility is managed by the Office of the Chief of Protocol and provides a secure and elegant setting for high-level diplomacy. At 70,000 square feet, it is larger than the Executive Residence of the White House and is often referred to as 'the world's most exclusive hotel.'
attractions.hours: Closed to the public (Visible from the exterior only)
attractions.admission: Not open to the public (Official Government Residence)
highlights: ['Site of the 1950 assassination attempt on President Harry S. Truman by Puerto Rican nationalists.', 'Served as the temporary residence for the Truman family during the White House reconstruction from 1948 to 1952.', "Location where Francis Preston Blair Sr. relayed Abraham Lincoln's offer to Robert E. Lee to command the Union forces in 1861.", 'The complex comprises four historic homes: Blair House, Lee House, Peter Parker House, and 704 Jackson Place.', 'Traditionally hosts the President-elect on the night before their inauguration.', 'Features the Blair-Lee Dining Room, a centerpiece for formal state dinners and diplomatic meetings.']
attractions.tips: As an active official residence, Blair House is not open for public tours. Visitors can view the historic facades of the four row houses from the sidewalk along Pennsylvania Avenue and Jackson Place. For safety and protocol, be prepared for visible security presence in the area. The best views are from Lafayette Square across the street.
attractions.nearby: The White House, Eisenhower Executive Office Building, Decatur House, Renwick Gallery, Lafayette Square

attractions.name: Bureau of Engraving and Printing (BEP)
attractions.description: The Bureau of Engraving and Printing (BEP) is the federal agency responsible for designing and producing United States currency, securities, and other government documents. Visitors can witness the production of paper money from an enclosed walkway above the production floor and explore exhibits on currency history and security.
attractions.address: 14th and C Streets, SW, Washington, D.C. 20228
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:30 a.m. – 3:15 p.m. (Last tour at 2:00 p.m., last Visitor Center entry at 2:45 p.m.); Extended Spring/Summer (March 23 - Sept 4): 8:30 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. (Last tour at 4:00 p.m., last Visitor Center entry at 4:30 p.m.)
attractions.admission: Free (tickets required for tours during peak season)
attractions.phone: (202) 874-2330 (Local Public Tours), 1-866-874-2330 (Toll-free Public Tours), (202) 874-3188 (Local General Inquiries)
highlights: ['Guided tour of the currency production floor', 'Introductory film about U.S. currency history', 'Exhibits on the design and security of paper money', 'Visitor Center with artifacts and historical displays', 'Gift shop featuring unique currency-related souvenirs']
attractions.tips: Tours are free but require tickets during peak months (March–August and around holidays), which can be reserved online in advance. No photography or filming is allowed inside the production areas. Arrive at least 10 minutes early to clear security screening.
attractions.nearby: U.S. Holocaust Memorial Museum, Washington Monument, Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History, National Mall, Tidal Basin

attractions.name: Capitol Hill
attractions.description: Capitol Hill is one of Washington, D.C.'s most historic and vibrant neighborhoods, serving as the seat of the United States government and a dense residential community. Famed for its 19th-century rowhouses and iconic landmarks like the U.S. Capitol and Supreme Court, the area also features a thriving commercial scene centered around Eastern Market and Barracks Row. It masterfully blends political significance with a charming, walkable atmosphere for residents and visitors alike.
attractions.hours: U.S. Capitol: Mon-Sat 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM; Library of Congress: Tue-Sat 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Thu until 8:00 PM); Eastern Market: Tue-Sat 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sun 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Folger Shakespeare Library: Tue-Sun 11:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Fri until 9:00 PM)
attractions.admission: Free (U.S. Capitol and Library of Congress require free timed-entry passes; Folger Shakespeare Library suggests a $15 donation)
highlights: ['U.S. Capitol Building and Visitor Center', 'Library of Congress (Thomas Jefferson Building)', 'Supreme Court of the United States Building', 'Eastern Market (Historic indoor and outdoor market)', 'Folger Shakespeare Library', 'Barracks Row (Historic dining and shopping district)']
attractions.tips: Plan ahead by reserving free timed-entry passes for the U.S. Capitol and Library of Congress online. Visit Eastern Market on weekends to experience the lively outdoor flea market and craft bazaar. Use public transportation via the Capitol South or Union Station Metro stops, as street parking is extremely limited. Explore the side streets to admire the historic Federal and Victorian-style rowhouses.
attractions.nearby: United States Botanic Garden, National Mall, Smithsonian Museums, Union Station, Navy Yard

attractions.name: Capitol Riverfront (Navy Yard)
attractions.description: The Capitol Riverfront is a vibrant, mixed-use waterfront neighborhood in Southeast Washington, D.C., situated between the U.S. Capitol and the Anacostia River. Recognized as D.C.'s fastest-growing neighborhood, it blends historic industrial charm with modern luxury residences, office spaces, and world-class green spaces like Yards Park and Canal Park. It serves as a major destination for sports, dining, and outdoor recreation, anchored by Nationals Park and Audi Field. (Washington.org, Capitol Riverfront)
attractions.hours: Neighborhood: Open 24/7; Yards Park: 6:00 AM - 9:00 PM daily; Canal Park: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM daily (Tripadvisor, Capitol Riverfront)
attractions.admission: Free (Capitol Riverfront)
highlights: ['Yards Park featuring an iconic bridge and dancing fountains', 'Nationals Park, home of the Washington Nationals MLB team', 'Audi Field, home of D.C. United MLS team', 'Canal Park with a seasonal outdoor ice skating rink and dancing fountains', 'Anacostia Riverwalk Trail for scenic waterfront strolling and biking', 'The Yards Marina for river access and water activities']
attractions.tips: The area is easily accessible via the Navy Yard-Ballpark Metro station on the Green Line. Visitors should check the seasonal events calendar for the popular Friday Night Concert Series at Yards Park. On game days at Nationals Park or Audi Field, expect high energy and crowds, making it a great time for dining but wise to book reservations in advance. (Capitol Riverfront)
attractions.nearby: U.S. Capitol Building, Library of Congress, Eastern Market, National Museum of the U.S. Navy (Washington.org)

attractions.name: Cherry Blossom Trees at the Tidal Basin (National Mall and Memorial Parks)
attractions.description: The blooming of the cherry trees around the Tidal Basin in Washington, DC has come to symbolize the natural beauty of our nation's capital city. These famous trees, a gift from Japan in 1912, signal the beginning of spring with an explosion of pale pink and white blossoms that surround the Tidal Basin. National Park Service
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily National Park Service
attractions.admission: Free National Park Service
highlights: 'Tidal Basin loop trail offering picturesque views of monuments framed by blossoms [Washington.org', 'Annual National Cherry Blossom Festival featuring parades, music, and fireworks National Cherry Blossom Festival', 'Sakura Matsuri Japanese Street Festival celebrating Japanese culture National Cherry Blossom Festival', 'Historical significance as a gift of friendship from Japan in 1912 National Park Service']
attractions.tips: Peak bloom usually occurs between late March and early April; use the Smithsonian Metro station for the easiest access to the Tidal Basin. Washington.org
attractions.nearby: Jefferson Memorial, Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial, Franklin Delano Roosevelt Memorial, Washington Monument Doyle Collection

attractions.name: Chesapeake and Ohio Canal National Historical Park
attractions.description: The Chesapeake and Ohio Canal National Historical Park preserves the remains of the C&O Canal, which operated from 1831 to 1924 as a lifeline for communities along the Potomac River. Today, the park stretches 184.5 miles from Washington, D.C. to Cumberland, Maryland, offering a pathway for discovering historical, natural, and recreational treasures (NPS).
attractions.hours: Sunrise to Sunset (Every Day) (NPS)
attractions.admission: Free (Great Falls area requires a fee: $20 Private Vehicle, $15 Motorcycle, $10 Per Person; Annual Pass $35) (NPS)
highlights: 'Hike or bike the 184.5-mile long towpath ([NPS)', 'Experience the Great Falls of the Potomac (NPS)', 'Take a mule-drawn canal boat ride at Great Falls or Georgetown (NPS)', 'Explore the engineering marvel of the 3,118-foot Paw Paw Tunnel (Visit Maryland)', 'Stay overnight in a historic lockhouse through the Canal Quarters program (C&O Canal Trust)', 'Visit the largest of the eleven aqueducts, the Monocacy Aqueduct (NPS)']
attractions.tips: The park is trash-free, so pack out everything you bring; bring plenty of water as well water at campgrounds may have iodine; wear sturdy footwear and a hat; check for current closures and river conditions before visiting (NPS).
attractions.nearby: Antietam National Battlefield, Glen Echo Park, Harpers Ferry National Historical Park, Rock Creek Park, Sugarloaf Mountain (Visit Maryland, C&O Canal Trust)

attractions.name: Cleveland Park Neighborhood / Cleveland Park Historic District Wikipedia
attractions.description: Cleveland Park is a historic residential neighborhood in Northwest Washington, D.C., named after President Grover Cleveland who once had a summer estate there. It is celebrated for its diverse 19th-century architecture, ranging from Queen Anne to Tudor Revival, and its vibrant commercial corridors along Connecticut and Wisconsin Avenues. Wikipedia
attractions.address: Connecticut Avenue NW (main corridor), Washington, DC 20008
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Neighborhood) Wikipedia
attractions.admission: Free (Public Neighborhood) Wikipedia
attractions.phone: (202) 827-6075 (Cleveland Park Historical Society) Cleveland Park Historical Society
highlights: 'Historic Art Deco Uptown Theater [Wikipedia', "Sam’s Park & Shop, one of the nation's first strip malls Cleveland Park Historical Society", "The Rosedale Conservancy, home to one of DC's oldest houses Cleveland Park Historical Society", 'Tregaron Conservancy and its Georgian estate grounds Cleveland Park Historical Society', 'Diverse architectural styles including Queen Anne, Shingle, and Mission Revival Wikipedia', 'Direct access to Rock Creek Park trails Conway Group']
attractions.tips: Visit the Rosedale Conservancy for a quiet escape in one of the city's oldest green spaces. Use the Cleveland Park Metro station (Red Line) for easy access to the heart of the neighborhood's commercial strip. Stroll through the side streets to appreciate the unique architectural details of the historic homes. Conway Group, DC Area Mom Collective
attractions.nearby: National Zoological Park (Smithsonian Zoo), Washington National Cathedral, Rock Creek Park, Woodley Park neighborhood Washingtonian

attractions.name: Dupont Circle National Park Service
attractions.category: Neighborhood, Park, and Landmark National Park Service, Dupont Circle BID
attractions.description: Dupont Circle is a historic neighborhood and roundabout park in Northwest Washington, D.C., centered around an iconic white marble fountain. It is renowned for its cosmopolitan flair, featuring a mix of grand historic mansions, vibrant nightlife, diverse dining, and a high concentration of international embassies. Washington.org, National Park Service
attractions.address: 1601 Connecticut Avenue NW, Suite 502 (BID Office); Intersection of Massachusetts, Connecticut, and New Hampshire Avenues (Park) Dupont Circle BID, National Park Service
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Open 24 hours National Park Service, Washington.org
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday National Park Service, Washington.org
attractions.admission: Free National Park Service, Washington.org
highlights: 'The iconic Dupont Circle Fountain, designed by Daniel Chester French and Henry Bacon [Washington.org', 'Embassy Row, a stretch of Massachusetts Avenue featuring dozens of diplomatic missions Wikipedia', 'The Phillips Collection, America’s first museum of modern art Washington.org', "The Sunday FRESHFARM Dupont Circle Market, one of the city's most popular farmers' markets Dupont Circle BID", 'Dupont Underground, a unique art and performance space located in a former trolley station Hotel Madera', 'The historic "Spanish Steps" on 22nd Street, a popular spot for picnics and photos Washington.org']
attractions.tips: Visit on Sunday mornings for the year-round farmers' market to experience the local culture. Use the Dupont Circle Metro station (Red Line) for easy access, and be sure to check out the 'First Friday' art walks where neighborhood galleries stay open late with special events. Washington.org, GW Today
attractions.nearby: The White House, Smithsonian National Zoological Park, Rock Creek Park, Adams Morgan neighborhood, and Logan Circle Washington.org, Lyle DC

attractions.name: East Potomac Park
attractions.description: East Potomac Park is a 330-acre man-made island and public park located between the Washington Channel and the Potomac River. The park is a major recreational hub in the capital, featuring extensive golf facilities, tennis courts, and scenic paths lined with iconic cherry trees. It culminates at Hains Point, a popular lookout where the Potomac and Anacostia rivers converge. Wikipedia, National Park Service
attractions.hours: Park: Open 24 hours daily. Golf Driving Range: 6:30 AM – 9:00 PM daily. Mini Golf: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM daily. National Park Service, playDCgolf
attractions.admission: Free for park entrance. Activity fees apply: Golf 18-holes ($42-$48), Mini-Golf ($15), Tennis (~$40/hr). Pool is free for DC residents. National Park Service, playDCgolf
highlights: ['East Potomac Golf Links with three courses and a driving range', 'Hains Point lookout at the southern tip of the island', 'The oldest continuously operating miniature golf course in the United States', 'Perimeter road popular for cycling, running, and walking with river views', 'Thousands of Japanese cherry trees that bloom in early spring', 'East Potomac Tennis Center and Aquatic Center pool']
attractions.tips: The park is best explored by bicycle or car as it is not directly served by a Metro station. The 4.4-mile perimeter loop is a favorite for local cyclists, so remain alert while driving. Visit during the Cherry Blossom Festival for some of the best floral displays in the city without the extreme crowds of the Tidal Basin. National Park Service, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Thomas Jefferson Memorial, Tidal Basin, The Wharf DC, International Spy Museum Wikipedia

attractions.name: East Potomac Park: Hains Point
attractions.description: Hains Point is a scenic recreational area located at the southern tip of East Potomac Park, where the Potomac River, Anacostia River, and Washington Channel converge. Named after Peter Conover Hains, the engineer who designed the Tidal Basin, the park is a favorite local destination for its waterfront views, cycling loops, and peaceful picnic spots away from the National Mall's main crowds. NPS, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 1100 Ohio Drive SW, Washington, DC 20242 NPS, Recreation.gov
attractions.hours: Open daily from sunrise to sunset; specific facilities like the pool and golf course have their own seasonal hours Airial Travel, Birders Guide MDDC
attractions.admission: Free entry to the park; fees apply for specific facilities such as golf, tennis, and the aquatic center NPS, KidFriendly DC
highlights: 'Confluence of the Potomac River, Anacostia River, and Washington Channel [NPS', '3-mile scenic loop trail popular for cycling, running, and walking Airial Travel', 'Extensive cherry blossom groves offering a quieter alternative to the Tidal Basin Yelp', 'Expansive picnic areas with over 500 free parking spaces NPS', 'Exceptional views of planes taking off and landing at Reagan National Airport Yelp', 'Proximity to the East Potomac Golf Links and East Potomac Aquatic Center Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Visit during the spring cherry blossom season for beautiful views with fewer crowds than the Tidal Basin. Be mindful of potential flooding during extreme high tides, and note that parking is free but can fill up quickly on weekends. Yelp, Airial Travel
attractions.nearby: Tidal Basin (Jefferson & FDR Memorials), The Wharf DC, East Potomac Golf Links, East Potomac Aquatic Center Wikipedia, Birders Guide MDDC

attractions.name: Eastern Market
attractions.description: Eastern Market is a historic public market in the Capitol Hill neighborhood that serves as a community hub and cultural destination. It features an indoor South Hall with diverse merchants, a seasonal open-air Farmers' Line, and a weekend outdoor market for local artists and antique dealers.
attractions.hours: Indoor Market: Tuesday-Saturday 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sunday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Outdoor Market: Tuesday 12:00 PM - 4:00 PM, Saturday-Sunday 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Monday: CLOSED
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['South Hall Market featuring fresh produce, meats, poultry, and baked goods', 'Weekend Outdoor Market with local artists, handmade crafts, and antiques', 'Fresh Tuesdays Farmers Market offering local produce and cheeses', 'The North Hall, a premier event space for community and private gatherings', 'Historic 19th-century brick architecture designed by Adolf Cluss', 'Vibrant atmosphere in the heart of the Capitol Hill neighborhood']
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekends to avoid crowds and try the famous blueberry buckwheat pancakes. Use the Eastern Market Metro station (Orange, Blue, and Silver lines) for the most convenient access. Note that while the indoor market is open all week (except Mondays), the full artisan and craft experience is best on Saturdays and Sundays.
attractions.nearby: U.S. Capitol Building, National Mall, Folger Shakespeare Library, Barracks Row (8th Street SE), Marine Barracks Washington, Library of Congress

attractions.name: Franklin Delano Roosevelt Memorial NPS
attractions.description: The Franklin Delano Roosevelt Memorial is a secluded site along the Tidal Basin that uses a sequence of five outdoor rooms to tell the story of FDR's presidency. Each room features pinkish carnelian granite walls, waterfalls, and bronze sculptures that evoke the emotions and challenges of his four terms in office, from the Great Depression to World War II NPS.
attractions.address: 400 West Basin Drive SW, Washington, DC 20002 NPS
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours every day NPS; Rangers on duty from 9:30 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. daily Washington.org
attractions.admission: Free NPS
highlights: "Five outdoor rooms representing FDR's prologue and four terms in office [NPS", 'Ten bronze sculptures including FDR with his dog Fala and a Depression-era breadline Wikipedia', 'Seven fountains and pools with over 100,000 gallons of flowing water NPS', "22 engraved quotations from Roosevelt's presidency carved into granite walls NPS", 'Statue of First Lady Eleanor Roosevelt, the first to be included in a presidential memorial Wikipedia', 'Wheelchair-accessible design throughout, including a statue of FDR in a wheelchair Washington.org']
attractions.tips: Visit at night to see the memorial beautifully illuminated and avoid the daytime crowds. Use the NPS app for a self-guided tour experience, and be sure to check out the 'Prologue Room' which was added to honor Roosevelt's disability Washington.org, NPS.
attractions.nearby: Martin Luther King, Jr. Memorial, Thomas Jefferson Memorial, Lincoln Memorial, Tidal Basin Washington.org

attractions.name: Freer Gallery of Art (Smithsonian Institution)
attractions.description: The Freer Gallery of Art, part of the Smithsonian's National Museum of Asian Art, houses one of the world's premier collections of Asian art alongside a significant collection of 19th-century American art. Founded by Charles Lang Freer, the museum features works from the Neolithic period to the early 20th century, including the world's most important collection of works by James McNeill Whistler (Smithsonian Institution, Britannica).

attractions.name: George Washington's Mount Vernon
attractions.description: George Washington's Mount Vernon is the historic home and plantation of the first President of the United States and his wife, Martha. Located on the banks of the Potomac River, the 21-room mansion is surrounded by lush gardens, original outbuildings, a working farm, and a museum dedicated to Washington's life and legacy. (Official Site, Wikivoyage))
attractions.address: 3200 Mount Vernon Memorial Highway, Mount Vernon, VA 22121
attractions.hours: April - October: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; November - March: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM. The museum typically remains open for 30 minutes to one hour after the estate closes. Shops and restaurants may have extended hours. (Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Open 365 days a year, including holidays. (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Adults (12+): $30; Youth (6-11): $16; Children (0-5): Free. Mansion Interior Tour requires a separate $2 reservation fee per ticket. Specialty tours (e.g., Expert-led) are additional (approx. $40). Military and teacher discounts available via ID.me ($4 off). Purple Heart recipients receive free admission. (Official Site, Official Site)
highlights: 'The 21-room Mansion, meticulously restored to its 1799 appearance.', 'The final resting place of George and Martha Washington at the New Tomb.', 'The Donald W. Reynolds Museum and Education Center featuring personal artifacts and immersive exhibits.', 'Lustrous gardens and landscapes, including the Upper and Lower Gardens designed by Washington.', 'A working 18th-century farm with rare-breed animals and daily demonstrations.', 'George Washington’s Distillery and Gristmill, located 2.7 miles from the main estate. ([Official Site)']
attractions.tips: Tickets should be purchased online in advance to save and guarantee entry. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the estate covers extensive ground with uneven paths. Plan to spend at least 3 hours to see the main highlights. Leashed dogs are welcome on the grounds but not inside the Mansion. (Official Site)

attractions.name: Georgetown Neighborhood
attractions.description: Georgetown is a historic neighborhood and commercial district in Northwest Washington, D.C., situated along the Potomac River. Founded in 1751, it is the city's oldest neighborhood and is renowned for its well-preserved colonial and Federal-style architecture and cobblestone streets. Today, it serves as a premier destination for high-end shopping, waterfront dining, and cultural landmarks like Georgetown University and the C&O Canal.
attractions.address: 1000 Potomac St. NW, Suite 122 (BID Office) / M St & Wisconsin Ave NW (Main Intersection)
attractions.hours: Neighborhood: Open 24/7. BID Office: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM. Old Stone House (Visitor Information): Wednesday–Sunday, 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM.
attractions.admission: Free to explore the neighborhood; specific attractions like Dumbarton Oaks or Tudor Place may have separate fees.
highlights: ['Chesapeake & Ohio (C&O) Canal and its historic towpath for walking and biking.', 'Historic architecture including the Old Stone House, the oldest standing building in DC.', 'Washington Harbour waterfront promenade with its fountain and river views.', "Georgetown University's iconic campus and Flemish Romanesque-style Healy Hall.", 'Dumbarton Oaks Research Library and its world-renowned public gardens.', 'Premier shopping and dining corridors along M Street and Wisconsin Avenue.']
attractions.tips: Use the DC Circulator or a water taxi for transport as there is no direct Metro station. Wear comfortable shoes for the cobblestone streets and steep hills like the famous 'Exorcist Steps.' Visit the waterfront at sunset for picturesque views of the Potomac River.
attractions.nearby: The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts, Rock Creek Park, Foggy Bottom, George Washington University.

This 800-acre park provides visitors with a stunning slice of nature and a glimpse into early American history through the remains of the Patowmack Canal National Park Service.

attractions.name: Hillwood Estate, Museum & Gardens
attractions.description: Hillwood Estate, Museum & Gardens is the former residence of businesswoman and philanthropist Marjorie Merriweather Post, featuring her world-renowned collection of Russian imperial art and French decorative arts. The 25-acre estate includes thirteen acres of formal gardens, a Japanese-style garden, and a Russian dacha, set against the backdrop of Rock Creek Park. It serves as a historic house museum dedicated to preserving Post's legacy and her exquisite taste in art and landscape design.
attractions.hours: Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Monday: Closed
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: Suggested donation: $20 for adults, $17 for seniors (65+), $10 for college students, $5 for children (6-18), and free for children under 6 and Hillwood members. A $3 discount is offered for weekday visits when reservations are made online. Free admission is also available for SNAP benefit recipients via Museums for All and for active-duty military personnel through Blue Star Museums.

The museum is a 10-15 minute walk from the Van Ness-UDC Metro station (Red Line), and free parking is available on-site (Washington.org).

attractions.name: Howard University
attractions.description: Howard University is a private, federally chartered historically black research university (HBCU) that serves as a premier center for academic excellence and culture (Howard University). Founded in 1867, it is the only HBCU designated as an R1 Carnegie research institution and is often affectionately referred to as "The Mecca" (Office of Admission). The campus spans 256 acres and is situated just two miles from the U.S. Capitol, placing it at the heart of national leadership and research (Howard University).

attractions.name: Korean War Veterans Memorial
attractions.description: The Korean War Veterans Memorial honors members of the U.S. Armed Forces who served and sacrificed during the Korean War from 1950 to 1953. Located southeast of the Lincoln Memorial, it features evocative statues, a massive wall of names, and a peaceful reflection pool (National Park Service, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily; Rangers are on duty to answer questions from 9:30 AM to 10:00 PM (National Park Service, Washington.org)
attractions.admission: Free (National Park Service)
highlights: ['The Column: 19 stainless steel statues representing a squad on patrol across rugged terrain.', 'Wall of Remembrance: A granite wall listing the names of over 36,000 Americans and 7,000 South Koreans who died in the war.', 'Mural Wall: A 164-foot-long black granite wall sandblasted with over 2,400 photographic images of supporting personnel and equipment.', 'Pool of Remembrance: A shallow circular pool surrounded by linden trees and benches for quiet reflection.', 'United Nations Wall: A low wall listing the 22 member states that contributed troops or medical support to the UN Command.', '"Freedom Is Not Free" inscription: A powerful silver-inlaid message on a granite wall near the pool.']
attractions.tips: The memorial is particularly moving when visited at night or in the early morning to avoid crowds and see the statues illuminated. Ranger-led interpretive programs are available throughout the day upon request (Washington.org, National Park Service).

attractions.name: Library of Congress
attractions.description: The Library of Congress is the largest library in the world and the oldest federal cultural institution in the United States, serving as the research arm of Congress. Housed in the magnificent 19th-century Thomas Jefferson Building, it preserves millions of items, including books, manuscripts, and historical treasures that document the American experience. Visitors can explore its stunning Italian Renaissance-style architecture and see iconic artifacts like the Gutenberg Bible.

attractions.name: Manassas National Battlefield Park (National Park Service)
attractions.description: Established in 1940, Manassas National Battlefield Park preserves over 5,000 acres of Virginia countryside that served as the site of two major American Civil War battles in 1861 and 1862. The park interprets the history of the First and Second Battles of Bull Run, which were pivotal moments that tested the resolve of both Union and Confederate forces. Visitors can explore the historic terrain where thousands fought and died, marked by monuments, trails, and preserved structures. (National Park Service)

attractions.description: The National Museum of American History collects, preserves, and displays the heritage of the United States in the areas of social, political, cultural, scientific, and military history. As the nation’s history museum, it aims to empower people to create a just and compassionate future by exploring the complexity of our past. The museum houses more than 1.8 million objects and three shelf-miles of archival collections that represent the nation’s diverse heritage. (Smithsonian Institution)

attractions.description: The National World War II Memorial honors the service of 16 million members of the U.S. Armed Forces, the support of millions on the home front, and the ultimate sacrifice of over 400,000 Americans. attractions.name: Thomas Jefferson Memorial
attractions.description: The Thomas Jefferson Memorial is a neoclassical monument dedicated to the third U.S. President and principal author of the Declaration of Independence. Situated on the Tidal Basin, the white-domed structure was inspired by the Pantheon in Rome and is famously framed by cherry blossom trees. Inside, a 19-foot bronze statue of Jefferson stands surrounded by inscriptions of his most significant writings on liberty and democracy (National Park Service, Old Town Trolley Tours).
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily; Rangers are on duty to answer questions from 9:30 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. (National Park Service)
attractions.days_open: Daily (365 days a year) (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: Free (National Park Service)
highlights: '19-foot bronze statue of Thomas Jefferson created by sculptor Rudolph Evans ([National Park Service)', "Neoclassical architecture inspired by the Roman Pantheon and Jefferson's own designs (Washington.org)", 'Inscriptions of key passages from the Declaration of Independence and other writings on the interior walls (Old Town Trolley Tours)', 'Stunning views of the Tidal Basin, especially during the annual Cherry Blossom Festival (National Park Service)', "Interior frieze quote: 'I have sworn upon the altar of God eternal hostility against every form of tyranny over the mind of man' (Old Town Trolley Tours)"]
attractions.tips: Visit early in the morning or late in the evening to avoid crowds and enjoy a more tranquil experience with beautiful lighting. The memorial is particularly scenic during the cherry blossom bloom in late March or early April, though this is the busiest time. Consider taking a stroll or a paddle boat around the Tidal Basin for the best photographic views of the structure reflected in the water (National Park Service, Old Town Trolley Tours).
attractions.nearby: Franklin Delano Roosevelt Memorial, Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial, George Mason Memorial, Tidal Basin paddle boats, Washington Monument (National Park Service, TripAdvisor)

attractions.name: Tidal Basin
attractions.description: The Tidal Basin is a 107-acre man-made reservoir and focal point of the National Mall and Memorial Parks. It is world-famous for its thousands of Japanese cherry trees and serves as a backdrop to iconic monuments including the Jefferson and Martin Luther King Jr. Memorials (NPS, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Cherry Blossom Watch, Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Cherry Blossom Watch, Trip.com)
highlights: 'Thousands of Japanese cherry trees blooming each spring ([NPS)', 'Tidal Basin Loop Trail offering views of major memorials (NPS)', 'Paddleboat rentals available for a water-based perspective (NPS)', 'Thomas Jefferson Memorial overlooking the water (Wikipedia)', 'Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial nearby (Wikipedia)', 'Franklin Delano Roosevelt Memorial along the trail (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Visit early in the morning or late at night to avoid crowds, especially during cherry blossom season (Trip Hacks DC). Use the Metro (Smithsonian or L'Enfant Plaza stations) for the easiest access (NPS). Make paddleboat reservations well in advance if visiting during peak bloom (Trip Hacks DC).
attractions.nearby: National Mall, Washington Monument, Jefferson Memorial, Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial, FDR Memorial, West Potomac Park (NPS)

attractions.name: Union Market (Union Market District)
attractions.description: Union Market is a historic food hall and culinary destination in Washington, D.C., featuring over 40 local artisanal vendors, restaurateurs, and boutiques. Originally opened in 1931 as a replacement for the old Center Market, the revitalized mid-century warehouse now anchors the vibrant Union Market District, offering everything from gourmet street food to a rooftop bar with stunning city views. Wikipedia, Union Market Official Site
attractions.hours: Monday–Saturday: 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Sunday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM Official Site
attractions.admission: Free (No admission fee; visitors pay per vendor for food and goods) Time Out
highlights: 'Over 40 independent merchants and food stalls under one roof [Official Site', 'Open-air rooftop bar and green space at Hi-Lawn Official Site', 'Yoko Ono’s "RELAX. IT’S ONLY LIFE" mural on the 6th Street wall Wikipedia', 'Regular events like the Drive-In at Union Market movie series Official Site', "Home to a diverse range of international cuisines including Laoban Dumplings, TaKorean, and Puddin' Washington.org", 'Curated boutique shopping at Salt & Sundry and high-performance knives at District Cutlery Washington.org']
attractions.tips: Come hungry to sample various stalls, but expect crowds on weekends. Check individual vendor websites for specific operating hours, as they may vary from the market's general hours. Don't miss the rooftop for great city views and a more relaxed atmosphere. Time Out, Washington.org
attractions.nearby: Gallaudet University Museum, National Postal Museum, Union Station, Metropolitan Branch Trail, and various local distilleries like Cotton & Reed. Tripadvisor, Washington.org

attractions.name: United States Botanic Garden
attractions.description: The United States Botanic Garden (USBG) is a living plant museum and the oldest continuously operating public garden in the United States, established by Congress in 1820. It serves as a center for plant conservation, education, and display, featuring diverse collections from tropical jungles to arid deserts. The garden highlights the importance of plants to human well-being and the Earth's ecosystems (Official Website).
attractions.hours: Conservatory: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM daily; National Garden (Gated Outdoor Gardens): 7:30 AM – 5:00 PM daily (Extended to 7:00 PM from April 1 – September 15); Bartholdi Fountain and Gardens: Dawn to dusk daily (Official Website).
attractions.admission: Free (no tickets required) (Official Website)
highlights: 'The Conservatory: A historic glass structure featuring the Tropics (with a canopy walk), Desert, and Orchid rooms. ([Official Website)', 'National Garden: A gated outdoor area containing the Rose Garden, Butterfly Garden, and the First Ladies Water Garden. (Official Website)', 'Bartholdi Fountain and Gardens: Featuring a landmark 30-foot fountain designed by Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi. (Official Website)', 'Orchid Collection: One of the largest and most diverse orchid collections in the United States. (Official Website)', 'Seasonal Holiday Show: A popular annual display featuring model trains and plant-based architectural models of D.C. landmarks. (Official Website)', 'Corpse Flower: The garden is famous for its periodic blooms of the rare and pungent Amorphophallus titanum. (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Take public transportation to Federal Center SW or L'Enfant Plaza Metro stations, as there is no dedicated parking. Be prepared for high humidity and occasional misting in the Conservatory's tropical zones. Personal photography is permitted, but commercial shoots require a permit. The facility is fully accessible to visitors with mobility needs (Official Website, Official Website).
attractions.nearby: United States Capitol, National Museum of the American Indian, National Gallery of Art, Library of Congress, Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum (TripAdvisor)

attractions.name: United States Capitol
attractions.description: The United States Capitol is the seat of the legislative branch of the federal government and a worldwide symbol of democracy. This neoclassical landmark houses the Senate and the House of Representatives and serves as a museum of American art and history. (U.S. Capitol Visitor Center, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: First St SE & East Capitol St, Washington, DC 20510
attractions.hours: Monday-Saturday: 8:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m.; Closed Sundays, Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's Day, and Inauguration Day. (U.S. Capitol Visitor Center)
attractions.admission: Free (U.S. Capitol Visitor Center)
highlights: ['The Rotunda: A historic circular room beneath the dome featuring massive paintings and the Apotheosis of Washington.', 'National Statuary Hall: A grand chamber displaying statues of prominent citizens representing each state.', 'The Crypt: The foundational center of the Capitol, featuring 40 Doric columns and historical exhibits.', "Exhibition Hall: An interactive space in the Visitor Center dedicated to the history of Congress and the building's evolution.", 'House and Senate Galleries: Public viewing areas to watch the legislative process when Congress is in session.']
attractions.tips: Book tour reservations at least 90 days in advance; arrive 45-60 minutes early for security; check the prohibited items list (no food or liquids); wear comfortable walking shoes. (U.S. Capitol Visitor Center)
attractions.nearby: Library of Congress, U.S. Supreme Court, U.S. Botanic Garden, National Mall, Smithsonian Museums (Washington.org)

attractions.name: United States Holocaust Memorial Museum
attractions.description: The United States Holocaust Memorial Museum is the nation’s official memorial to the Holocaust, providing for the documentation, study, and interpretation of Holocaust history. It serves as a living memorial that inspires citizens and leaders worldwide to confront hatred, prevent genocide, and promote human dignity. United States Holocaust Memorial Museum, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 100 Raoul Wallenberg Place, SW, Washington, DC 20024-2126
attractions.hours: 10 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. daily (Exhibitions and shop close at 5:20 p.m.; Last entry to Permanent Exhibition at 4:30 p.m.) United States Holocaust Memorial Museum
attractions.days_open: Every day (Except Yom Kippur and Christmas Day) United States Holocaust Memorial Museum
attractions.admission: Free (A $1 transaction fee applies per ticket for online reservations through Etix) United States Holocaust Memorial Museum, Etix
highlights: 'Permanent Exhibition: The Holocaust (a narrative history featuring artifacts and personal stories) [United States Holocaust Memorial Museum', 'Americans and the Holocaust (examining American responses to Nazism and war) United States Holocaust Memorial Museum', "Remember the Children: Daniel's Story (history told through a child's experience, suitable for ages 8+) United States Holocaust Memorial Museum", 'One Thousand and Seventy-eight Blue Skies (images of skies above concentration camps) United States Holocaust Memorial Museum', 'Antisemitism: Ancient Hatred, Modern Violence (installation on the history of antisemitism) United States Holocaust Memorial Museum']
attractions.tips: Advance timed-entry tickets are required for the Permanent Exhibition from March through August; reserve online to guarantee entry. Allow at least 1 to 3 hours for the Permanent Exhibition. The museum is fully accessible, and a cafe and shop are available on-site. United States Holocaust Memorial Museum, Washington.org
attractions.nearby: Bureau of Engraving and Printing, Washington Monument, National Museum of African American History and Culture, Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History, International Spy Museum TripAdvisor

attractions.name: Vietnam Veterans Memorial
attractions.description: The Vietnam Veterans Memorial is a powerful tribute to the men and women who served in the United States Armed Forces during the Vietnam War. Its primary feature is the Memorial Wall, a 493-foot-long black granite structure engraved with the names of over 58,000 service members who died or remain missing. The site serves as a place of healing and reflection, harmonizing with its surroundings near the Lincoln Memorial. (National Park Service) (Vietnam Veterans Memorial Fund)
attractions.address: 5 Henry Bacon Drive NW, Washington, DC 20002
attractions.hours: Monday: 12:00 AM – 11:59 PM; Tuesday: 12:00 AM – 11:59 PM; Wednesday: 12:00 AM – 11:59 PM; Thursday: 12:00 AM – 11:59 PM; Friday: 12:00 AM – 11:59 PM; Saturday: 12:00 AM – 11:59 PM; Sunday: 12:00 AM – 11:59 PM (Note: Rangers on duty 9:30 AM – 10:00 PM daily except Dec 25) (National Park Service) (Washington.org)
attractions.admission: Free (National Park Service)
highlights: 'The Memorial Wall (The Wall) featuring 58,318 names in chronological order ([National Park Service)', 'The Three Servicemen statue, providing a realistic depiction of soldiers from the era (National Park Service)', "The Vietnam Women's Memorial, dedicated to the 265,000 women who served (National Park Service)", 'The In Memory plaque, honoring those who died after the war due to their service (Vietnam Veterans Memorial Fund)', 'Maya Lin\'s minimalist design, symbolic of a "wound that is closed and healing" (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Visit during early morning or late evening for a more quiet and contemplative experience. Rangers and volunteers are on-site daily to assist with finding names and providing name rubbings. Because parking is extremely limited, using the Metrorail (Foggy Bottom or Federal Triangle stations) is highly recommended. (National Park Service) (Washington.org)
attractions.nearby: Lincoln Memorial, Korean War Veterans Memorial, Washington Monument, Constitution Gardens, Albert Einstein Memorial (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Washington Monument
attractions.description: The Washington Monument is a 555-foot marble obelisk built to honor George Washington, the first President of the United States. Located on the National Mall, it is the world's tallest free-standing stone structure and offers sweeping views of the nation's capital from its observation level. National Park Service, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. (Last tour entry at 4:30 p.m.) National Park Service
attractions.days_open: Daily, except July 4 and December 25 National Park Service
attractions.admission: Free (A non-refundable $1.00 service fee applies per advance ticket reserved online) National Park Service
highlights: "World's tallest free-standing stone structure, standing at 555 feet 5-1/8 inches [Recreation.gov", 'Observation deck at the 500-foot level offering 360-degree views of Washington, D.C. Recreation.gov', "Museum at the 490-foot level with exhibits on the monument's construction and history Recreation.gov", 'Distinctive two-toned exterior caused by a 22-year pause in construction Recreation.gov', 'Interior walls lined with 193 commemorative stones donated by states, cities, and foreign nations Recreation.gov']
attractions.tips: Advance tickets are highly recommended and can be reserved online at Recreation.gov 30 days in advance or the day before at 10:00 a.m. ET. A limited number of free same-day walk-up tickets are available at the Washington Monument Lodge starting at 8:45 a.m. daily. Arrive at least 15 minutes before your scheduled time for security screening. National Park Service, Recreation.gov
attractions.nearby: National Museum of African American History and Culture, World War II Memorial, Lincoln Memorial, Smithsonian Castle, National Museum of American History Tripadvisor, National Park Service

attractions.name: Washington National Cathedral (Cathedral Church of Saint Peter and Saint Paul)
attractions.description: The Washington National Cathedral, formally the Cathedral Church of Saint Peter and Saint Paul, is a majestic Neo-Gothic structure located at the highest point in Washington, D.C. It serves as a national house of prayer, hosting major national events, and is renowned for its stunning architecture, featuring 231 stained glass windows and over 1,000 stone grotesques.
attractions.hours: Sightseeing: Typically Mon-Fri 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Sun 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM (Subject to change; check website calendar). Worship: Daily morning and evening services. Official Calendar
attractions.days_open: Daily (Sightseeing hours vary daily; worship services occur daily)
attractions.admission: Adults: $17.00, Youth (5-12): $12.00, Ages 4 and under: Free. Worship services and private prayer are free. Official Site
highlights: ['The Space Window, which contains a piece of lunar rock from the Apollo 11 mission.', 'The Canterbury Pulpit, the site where Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his final Sunday sermon.', 'The Darth Vader grotesque, located on the northwest tower (visible via binoculars).', 'The beautiful Bishop’s Garden, a 59-acre medieval-style space designed by Frederick Law Olmsted Jr.', 'The Crypt Level, which houses the final resting places of Woodrow Wilson and Helen Keller.', 'Panoramic views of D.C. from the Tower Observation Gallery.']
attractions.tips: Download the Bloomberg Connects app for a digital guide and audio tours. Sightseeing tickets are required for interior exploration outside of worship. Dress respectfully as it is an active house of worship. Check the online calendar before visiting as hours frequently change for special events. Visit Tips
attractions.nearby: Bishop’s Garden, Olmsted Woods, Cleveland Park, Glover Archbold Park, Smithsonian National Zoological Park.

attractions.name: Washington Union Station
attractions.category: Landmark, Transportation Hub, Shopping, and Dining
attractions.description: Union Station D.C. is a superbly restored, historic, mixed-use, intermodal transportation and shopping center located just blocks from the U.S. Capitol Building. Designed by Daniel Burnham and opened in 1907, it serves as a major gateway to the city and a premier leisure destination with three levels of shopping and dining. Union Station D.C., Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours to ticketed passengers; closed to the general public daily from 11:00 PM to 5:00 AM. Retail Stores: Mon-Sat 10:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Sun 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM. Food Court: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Sun 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM. Union Station D.C.
attractions.admission: Free to enter the station; ticket prices for Amtrak, MARC, VRE, and Metro services vary by destination and class. Amtrak, Washington Union Station
highlights: ['Historic Beaux-Arts architecture designed by Daniel Burnham', 'Major transit hub for Amtrak, MARC, VRE, and the DC Metro Red Line', 'Extensive retail concourse with over 50 shops and restaurants', 'Grand Main Hall featuring 96-foot vaulted ceilings and gold leaf detailing', 'Columbus Memorial Fountain and Plaza located directly in front of the station', 'The National Postal Museum situated immediately adjacent to the station building']
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30-45 minutes before train departures to navigate the gates. Store and restaurant hours may vary individually, so check specific listings in the directory. During the late-night closure (11 PM - 5 AM), only ticketed passengers with valid identification are permitted inside. Use the lower-level food court for a wide variety of quick-service meals. Union Station D.C., Amtrak Guide
attractions.nearby: U.S. Capitol Building, National Postal Museum, National Mall, Supreme Court of the United States, Library of Congress. Reddit, Trolley Tours

attractions.name: Blair House (The President's Guest House) Blair House Official Site
attractions.category: Landmark, Historic House, Government Guest House Blair House Official Site, GSA
attractions.description: Blair House, known as the President's Guest House, is a 120-room complex that serves as the official residence for foreign dignitaries visiting the President of the United States. Located across from the White House, it consists of four interconnected 19th-century houses and has played a vital role in American diplomacy since 1942. Blair House Official Site, GSA
attractions.address: 1651 Pennsylvania Ave NW, Washington, DC 20503-0005 Tripadvisor, White House Historical Association
attractions.city: Washington, DC Blair House Official Site
attractions.hours: Not open for public tours Blair House Official Site, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: None (Closed to the public) Blair House Official Site
attractions.admission: Free; Not open to the public Blair House Official Site, Tripadvisor
highlights: 'Official guest residence for foreign heads of state visiting the U.S. President [Blair House Official Site', 'Comprised of four historic townhouses: Blair House, Lee House, and two Jackson Place buildings GSA', 'Served as the temporary White House for President Harry S. Truman from 1948 to 1952 Simple Wikipedia', 'Site of the 1950 assassination attempt on President Truman by Puerto Rican nationalists Blair House Official Site', 'Contains 119 rooms, including 14 guest bedrooms and 35 bathrooms, totaling 70,000 square feet GSA', "Home to Andrew Jackson’s 'Kitchen Cabinet' during his presidency Blair House Official Site"]
attractions.tips: Since the house is not open for public tours, the best way to see it is by walking along Pennsylvania Avenue or Lafayette Square. Look for the flags of visiting dignitaries flying from the house when a foreign leader is in residence. Tripadvisor, TRIP.COM

attractions.name: Capital One Arena
attractions.description: Capital One Arena is a premier sports and entertainment destination in the heart of Washington, D.C., serving as the home for the NBA's Washington Wizards and the NHL's Washington Capitals. The venue hosts over 200 events annually, ranging from major concert tours and family shows to world-class sporting events, and is currently undergoing a massive $850 million renovation to further modernize the fan experience.
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday - Friday: 12 PM - 4 PM. Event Days: 12 PM until approximately 30-60 minutes after the start of the event. (Note: The Box Office is typically closed for the summer season starting in late April).
attractions.days_open: Open Monday through Friday for Box Office services and on all scheduled event days.
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event (NBA, NHL, concerts). Typically includes Standard, Club, and Suite seating tiers. Tickets can be purchased via Ticketmaster or the venue Box Office.
highlights: ['Home of the Washington Wizards (NBA) and Washington Capitals (NHL)', 'Hosts over 200 events per year, including major concerts and family shows', 'Directly situated atop the Gallery Place-Chinatown Metro station for easy access', 'Undergoing an $850 million multi-year renovation project through 2028', 'Features the first brick-and-mortar sportsbook in a U.S. professional sports venue (Caesars Sportsbook)', 'Located in the vibrant Penn Quarter and Chinatown neighborhood']
attractions.tips: Capital One Arena has a strict no-bag policy; only small clutches (5" x 7") or medical/parenting bags (14" x 14" x 6") are permitted. Capital One cardholders can often utilize a dedicated entrance to skip lines. The venue is completely cashless, so ensure you have a credit or debit card for all concession and retail purchases. Using the CLEAR app can also help expedite entry into the arena.
attractions.nearby: National Portrait Gallery, Smithsonian American Art Museum, Chinatown Arch, Ford's Theatre, National Building Museum

attractions.name: Decatur House (Stephen Decatur House)
attractions.description: Decatur House is a historic red brick Federal-style townhouse and National Historic Landmark located on Lafayette Square near the White House. Built in 1818 for naval hero Commodore Stephen Decatur, it serves as a museum and the home of the David M. Rubenstein National Center for White House History. It is notable for being one of the oldest surviving homes in the capital and for featuring one of the few remaining urban slave quarters in the United States.
attractions.address: 748 Jackson Place NW (also 1610 H Street NW)
attractions.hours: Mondays: 10:30 AM and 1:00 PM (Public tour times); Tuesday-Friday: By appointment for private tours; Saturday-Sunday: Closed to the public.
attractions.days_open: Mondays (Open for public tours); Tuesday-Friday (Private tours by request)
attractions.admission: Free (Reservation via Eventbrite required for public tours)
highlights: ['Designed by Benjamin Henry Latrobe, America’s first professional architect and engineer.', 'Home to the David M. Rubenstein National Center for White House History.', 'Contains the only preserved urban slave quarters within sight of the White House.', 'The first private residence built in the White House neighborhood.', 'Successively housed prominent figures like Henry Clay and Martin Van Buren.', 'Features a mix of 19th-century and 20th-century interior styles.']
attractions.tips: Public tours are only offered on Mondays and require a free reservation through Eventbrite. Street parking is extremely limited near the White House; using nearby garages or the Metro (Farragut West or Farragut North) is recommended. Private group tours can be requested for Tuesday through Friday.
attractions.nearby: Lafayette Square, The White House, St. John’s Episcopal Church, Renwick Gallery, Eisenhower Executive Office Building

attractions.name: Ford's Theatre National Historic Site
attractions.description: Ford’s Theatre National Historic Site preserves the location where President Abraham Lincoln was assassinated on April 14, 1865 (NPS). The site includes the historic theatre, a museum detailing Lincoln’s presidency and the assassination conspiracy, and the Petersen House where he passed away (Ford's Theatre). It remains an active professional theatre that celebrates Lincoln’s legacy through performances and educational programming (Ford's Theatre).
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Sunday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM. (Timed entry begins every half hour from 9:00 AM to 4:30 PM; specific availability may vary due to matinee performances) (Ford's Theatre, WhichMuseum)
attractions.days_open: Open daily (Closed Thanksgiving Day and December 25)
attractions.admission: Admission to the historic site is free, though a $5.00 non-refundable convenience fee is required for advance timed-entry reservations made online (NPS, Ford's Theatre). A limited number of free same-day tickets are available at the box office starting at 8:30 a.m. on a first-come, first-served basis (Ford's Theatre). Optional audio tours (Acoustiguide) are available for $5.00 (Ford's Theatre).
highlights: The Historic Theatre and the President’s Box, preserved to look as it did in 1865 (Ford's Theatre)
Ford’s Theatre Museum featuring the Deringer pistol used by John Wilkes Booth and Lincoln's clothing (Ford's Theatre)
The Petersen House, also known as the 'House Where Lincoln Died' (NPS)
Aftermath Exhibits including a 34-foot tower comprised of more than 15,000 books about Abraham Lincoln (Ford's Theatre)
Seasonal performances of 'One Destiny,' a one-act play about the night of the assassination (Ford's Theatre)
attractions.tips: Advance reservations are highly recommended as tickets often sell out weeks in advance (Ford's Theatre). Visitors should allow 2–3 hours for a full tour of the theatre, museum, and Petersen House (Ford's Theatre). Always check the performance calendar before visiting, as the theatre interior is often closed to tourists during rehearsals and matinees (NPS).
attractions.nearby: Petersen House, National Portrait Gallery, Smithsonian American Art Museum, National Archives Museum, International Spy Museum (Tripadvisor, Evendo)

attractions.name: International Monetary Fund Visitor Center (IMF Center)
attractions.description: The IMF Visitor Center, also known as the IMF Center, serves as a public venue for economics education and engagement in the heart of Washington, D.C. It offers permanent and rotating exhibits that detail the history of the international monetary system and the Fund's role in promoting global financial stability. The center also features a bookstore and an auditorium that hosts public economic forums, book discussions, and educational briefings. (IMF Center)
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:30 AM – 6:00 PM, Tuesday: 8:30 AM – 6:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:30 AM – 6:00 PM, Thursday: 8:30 AM – 6:00 PM, Friday: 8:30 AM – 6:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed (IMF Business Hours)
attractions.admission: Free (IMF Center)
highlights: 'Permanent exhibit explaining the history and function of the international monetary system ([IMF Exhibits)', 'The Ancient Trade Route Collection featuring historic gold and silver measures of value (IMF Exhibits)', 'IMF Bookstore and Gift Shop offering specialized publications and souvenirs (IMF Guide)', 'Auditorium hosting frequent public book forums and economic briefings (IMF Events)', 'Interactive educational materials designed for students and educators (IMF Center)']
attractions.tips: Visitors must present a valid government-issued photo ID and undergo security screening including magnetometers and x-rays (IMF Visitor Access). Note that the center may have restricted access or closures during the IMF/World Bank Annual and Spring Meetings (IMF Connect). It is easily accessible via the Farragut West and Farragut North Metro stations (IMF Map).
attractions.nearby: The White House, World Bank Headquarters, Renwick Gallery, George Washington University, Corcoran Gallery of Art (Tripadvisor, Expedia)

attractions.name: Lafayette Square (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Lafayette Square is a seven-acre public park located within President's Park in Washington, D.C., directly north of the White House. Named for the Marquis de Lafayette, a hero of the American Revolutionary War, it is a National Historic Landmark surrounded by historic buildings and frequently serves as a site for public protests. (Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (Note: Grounds of President's Park are open year-round, subject to temporary closures by the U.S. Secret Service) (Tripadvisor, NPS)
attractions.admission: Free (Viator)
highlights: 'Andrew Jackson Equestrian Statue (first bronze statue cast in the U.S.) ([Wikipedia)', 'Statues of foreign Revolutionary War heroes: Lafayette, Rochambeau, von Steuben, and Kościuszko (Wikipedia)', "Historic surrounding buildings: St. John's Episcopal Church and Decatur House (White House Historical Association)", 'Close proximity to the White House for excellent photo opportunities (Washington.org)', 'Historically served as a graveyard, apple orchard, and slave market (Wikipedia)', 'Designated a National Historic Landmark in 1970 (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: The park is a great spot for taking photos of the White House and is wheelchair and stroller accessible. Visitors should be aware that the park is frequently used for protests and may have temporary security closures due to its proximity to the White House. (Viator, NPS)
attractions.nearby: White House, Decatur House, St. John's Episcopal Church, Renwick Gallery, Treasury Building (Washington.org)

attractions.name: National Archives Museum
attractions.description: The National Archives Museum is the permanent home of the United States' most important founding documents, collectively known as the Charters of Freedom. Housed in a neoclassical building designed by John Russell Pope, the museum offers a profound look at American history through billions of records and interactive exhibits. It serves as both a public museum and a major research center for federal government and historical records. National Archives, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM. (Last admission 30 minutes before closing). National Archives Museum

attractions.name: National Building Museum
attractions.description: The National Building Museum is America's leading cultural institution devoted to the history and impact of the built environment. Housed in the historic 19th-century Pension Building, it inspires curiosity about the world we design and build through immersive exhibitions and educational programs. National Building Museum, Pentagram

Located in the heart of Washington, D.C., it celebrates a legacy of global discovery through iconic photography, interactive galleries, and a world-class theater. Visitors can explore thematic zones like the Rolex Explorers Landing and the immersive Photo Ark exhibition, all designed to ignite curiosity and inspire stewardship of the planet. (Official Website, Official Website)

attractions.name: National Law Enforcement Officers Memorial
attractions.description: The National Law Enforcement Officers Memorial is the nation's monument dedicated to honoring federal, state, and local law enforcement officers who have died in the line of duty. Dedicated in 1991, the memorial features two curving limestone walls inscribed with the names of more than 24,000 fallen officers, serving as a solemn place for reflection and remembrance. (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours a day, 365 days a year (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: 'Two 304-foot-long curving blue-gray limestone walls inscribed with over 24,000 names of fallen officers. ([Official Website)', 'Four bronze lion statues sculpted by Raymond Kaskey, symbolizing the protective role of law enforcement. (Official Website)', 'A central plaza with an intricate paving pattern and a bronze medallion of a blue shield with a red rose. (Official Website)', '"Pathways of Remembrance" lined with trees and name panels where tributes are frequently left. (Official Website)', 'Annual addition of new names every spring in conjunction with National Police Week ceremonies. (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: The memorial is open 24/7 and is particularly moving when lit at night. Visitors can use directories at each of the four entrances to locate specific names by panel and line number. Rubbing names is permitted, and paper and pencils are typically provided at the directory stands. (Official Website)

Located on the site of the former Pershing Park, the memorial features a 58-foot-long bronze sculpture titled 'A Soldier’s Journey,' which portrays the transformation of an American soldier through combat and return home. The site also includes a statue of General John J. Pershing, cascading water features, and historical inscriptions. National Park Service

Operating for nearly 100 years, the canal served as a vital lifeline for communities, and today it offers a wealth of historical, natural, and recreational treasures for visitors to explore. (NPS)

Currently housing the Corcoran School of the Arts and Design at George Washington University, the building serves as a vibrant center for art education and public exhibitions. It is widely recognized for its grand architectural features and its long-standing role as the city's oldest private cultural institution (Corcoran School of the Arts & Design, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Dumbarton Oaks Research Library and Collection (Dumbarton Oaks)
attractions.category: Museum, Garden, and Research Institute (Dumbarton Oaks)
attractions.description: Dumbarton Oaks is a historic estate and research institute in Georgetown, Washington, D.C., gifted to Harvard University by Robert and Mildred Bliss. It is world-renowned for its specialized collections of Byzantine and Pre-Columbian art and its sixteen acres of formal terraced gardens designed by Beatrix Farrand. The site is also famous for hosting the 1944 Dumbarton Oaks Conference, which laid the groundwork for the United Nations (Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Theodore Roosevelt Island National Memorial
attractions.description: Theodore Roosevelt Island is an 88.5-acre memorial to the 26th U.S. President, located in the Potomac River. Transformed in the 1930s from neglected farmland into a 'real forest,' the island features a memorial plaza with a 17-foot bronze statue and miles of trails through diverse habitats including wooded uplands and swampy bottomlands (National Park Service).
