attractions.hours: Every Day: 6:00 AM–10:00 PM (National Park Service)
attractions.days_open: Open 365 days a year (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: Free; no entrance pass required (National Park Service)
highlights: '17-foot bronze statue of Theodore Roosevelt in the memorial plaza ([Wikipedia)', "Four massive granite tablets inscribed with Roosevelt's conservation philosophy (Simply Awesome Trips)", 'The 1.5-mile Swamp Trail featuring a boardwalk over cattail marshes (National Park Service)', 'Extensive wildlife viewing opportunities, including ospreys and other river birds (National Park Service)', 'Direct access to the northern terminus of the 18-mile Mount Vernon Trail (National Park Service)']
attractions.tips: Bicycles are not permitted on the island but can be parked at racks near the footbridge. The island is accessible by car only from the northbound lanes of the George Washington Memorial Parkway. It is highly recommended to bring bug spray during the summer months and note that restrooms on the island are typically closed from late fall until spring (Simply Awesome Trips, National Park Service).

Today, it serves as a bustling entertainment district celebrated for its unparalleled nightlife, live music venues, iconic murals, and rich African American heritage.

attractions.admission: Free (individual venues and attractions may have separate fees; the African American Civil War Museum is free with a suggested donation)
highlights: ['Ben’s Chili Bowl (legendary eatery serving signature half-smokes)', 'African American Civil War Memorial and Museum', 'Historic Lincoln Theatre and Howard Theatre', 'Greater U Street Heritage Trail (self-guided walking tour)', 'Vibrant street art and murals celebrating jazz legends', 'Birthplace of Duke Ellington landmarks']
attractions.tips: Use the U Street/African-Amer Civil War Memorial/Cardozo Metro station for easy access. Visit on a Sunday afternoon to experience the community drum circle at nearby Meridian Hill Park. Follow the 14 illustrated signs of the Heritage Trail to discover the neighborhood's deep historical roots.
attractions.nearby: Meridian Hill Park (Malcolm X Park), Howard University, Logan Circle, Adams Morgan, Shaw neighborhood

attractions.name: Gallaudet University
attractions.description: Gallaudet University is the world's only university specifically designed for deaf and hard of hearing students, offering a unique bilingual environment in American Sign Language and English. Established in 1864, the 99-acre campus, known as Kendall Green, features a stunning blend of 19th-century Victorian Gothic architecture and modern DeafSpace design. It serves as a global cultural hub and a preeminent resource for deaf history, language, and research (Gallaudet University, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed (Gallaudet University)
attractions.admission: Free (Gallaudet University)
highlights: ['The National Deaf Life Museum, showcasing the rich history and culture of the deaf community.', 'Kendall Green, the historic 99-acre campus designed by Frederick Law Olmsted and Calvert Vaux.', 'DeafSpace architecture, featuring innovative design elements that facilitate visual communication.', 'Historic 19th-century buildings like Chapel Hall and College Hall, which are National Historic Landmarks.', "The world's most complete library collection of materials related to deaf people and culture.", 'Guided and self-guided tours conducted in American Sign Language with voice interpretation available.']
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to pick up a self-guided tour brochure at the Maguire Welcome Center in the Edward Miner Gallaudet Memorial Building. For guided tours, it is best to schedule in advance to ensure voice interpreters are available if you do not sign fluently. The campus is extremely walkable and bike-friendly, located just blocks from the NoMa-Gallaudet U Metro station (Gallaudet University).
attractions.nearby: Union Market, H Street Corridor, NoMa Murals, U.S. Capitol, National Mall (Gallaudet University)

attractions.name: H Street Corridor (Atlas District)
attractions.description: The H Street Corridor is a vibrant 1.5-mile stretch in Northeast Washington, D.C., known for its eclectic mix of nightlife, award-winning restaurants, and a flourishing arts scene. Rebuilt from the ground up after the 1968 riots, it has transformed into one of the city's most dynamic commercial and residential hubs, anchored by the historic Atlas Performing Arts Center. The neighborhood offers a unique blend of historic row houses, modern luxury apartments, and a communal atmosphere that reflects the "heart of the city." (hstreet.org, washington.org)
attractions.hours: Neighborhood: Open 24/7; H Street Main Street Office: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (hstreet.org)
attractions.admission: Free (washington.org)
highlights: ['Atlas Performing Arts Center
A creative hub for theater, dance, and music (hstreet.org)', 'Annual H Street Festival
One of the largest street festivals in the country with over 150,000 attendees (washington.org)', "H Street Farmers' Market
Seasonal Saturday market featuring local produce and goods (washington.org)", 'Little Ethiopia
A concentration of acclaimed Ethiopian restaurants and businesses (washington.org)', 'Linden Court Murals
An "art gallery" in an alleyway depicting D.C.\'s culture (washington.org)', 'Maketto
A 6,000-square-square-foot communal marketplace and restaurant (washington.org)']
attractions.tips: While the DC Streetcar officially ceased operations in March 2026, visitors can utilize the D20 bus or Metrobus X2 route as efficient alternatives to navigate the corridor. Visit during the fall for the annual H Street Festival to experience the neighborhood's peak cultural energy, and explore the 'Hidden Gems' like the Binge Bar for unique, spirit-free nightlife experiences. (WJLA, hstreet.org)
attractions.nearby: Union Station, Union Market, U.S. Capitol, NoMa neighborhood (Compass)

attractions.name: Kenilworth Park & Aquatic Gardens
attractions.description: Kenilworth Park & Aquatic Gardens is a National Park Service site that preserves a unique collection of water lilies and lotuses in cultivated ponds. Located along the Anacostia River, it features the largest remaining tidal marsh in Washington, D.C., offering a tranquil oasis for nature lovers and birdwatchers (Wikipedia, National Park Service).
attractions.hours: Park Grounds: Daily 8:00 AM–4:00 PM (Summer Wednesdays–Sundays until 8:00 PM); Visitor Center: Daily 9:00 AM–3:00 PM (Summer Wednesdays–Sundays until 7:00 PM) (National Park Service)
attractions.days_open: Open daily except for New Year's Day, Thanksgiving, and Christmas Day (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: Free (National Park Service)
highlights: 'Vibrant summer blooms of exotic lotus and water lilies ([National Park Service)', 'Extensive tidal marsh and wetland ecosystem boardwalk (Friends of Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens)', 'Over 240 species of birds for year-round birdwatching (National Park Service)', 'Annual Lotus and Water Lily Festival in July (Washingtonian)', 'Access to the scenic Anacostia Riverwalk Trail for biking and hiking (KidFriendly DC)']
attractions.tips: Visit in early morning during summer (mid-June to mid-August) to see lilies in full bloom before they close in the afternoon heat. Wear comfortable shoes as trails can be muddy, and bring water and insect repellent (Free Tours by Foot, Friends of Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens).
attractions.nearby: U.S. National Arboretum, Anacostia Park, Bladensburg Waterfront Park (Tripadvisor, Birders Guide MD/DC)

attractions.name: United States National Arboretum
attractions.description: The United States National Arboretum is a 446-acre botanical research center and public park in Northeast Washington, D.C. It features diverse gardens and collections, including the iconic National Capitol Columns and the National Bonsai & Penjing Museum. Operating under the USDA, it serves as a major center for plant-based research and conservation while providing a serene outdoor escape for visitors.
attractions.hours: Arboretum Grounds: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM daily; National Bonsai & Penjing Museum: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM daily (except certain federal holidays); Administration Building: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM daily.
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['National Capitol Columns', 'National Bonsai & Penjing Museum', 'Azalea Collections', 'National Grove of State Trees', 'National Herb Garden', 'Bald Eagle Nest Cam (Mr. President and The First Lady)']
attractions.tips: Arrive early on spring weekends to avoid crowds at the azaleas; note that the R Street entrance is closed after 2:00 PM on weekdays; parking is free and abundant on the grounds.
attractions.nearby: United States Capitol, Anacostia River, Kenilworth Park & Aquatic Gardens, H Street Corridor

attractions.name: Arlington National Cemetery
attractions.description: Arlington National Cemetery is the nation's most prestigious military cemetery, serving as the final resting place for more than 400,000 service members and their families. Established during the Civil War in 1864, the 639-acre grounds offer a powerful tribute to American history and sacrifice. Wikipedia
attractions.address: 1 Memorial Avenue, Arlington, VA 22211 Official Website
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sunday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM Official Website
attractions.admission: Free entry (Timed-entry passes may be required/recommended); Parking and tram tours have separate fees Hotel Washington Guide
highlights: ['Tomb of the Unknown Soldier (Changing of the Guard)', 'John F. Kennedy Gravesite and Eternal Flame', 'Arlington House (The Robert E. Lee Memorial)', 'Memorial Amphitheater', 'Space Shuttle Challenger and Columbia Memorials', 'Women in Military Service for America Memorial']
attractions.tips: Stay hydrated and bring water as the grounds are vast; Wear comfortable walking shoes; Maintain a quiet and respectful demeanor; 100% ID check is required for entry; Food and alcohol are prohibited. Official Website
attractions.nearby: Lincoln Memorial, U.S. Marine Corps War Memorial (Iwo Jima), The Pentagon, LBJ Memorial Grove Hotel Washington Guide

attractions.name: National 9/11 Pentagon Memorial Pentagon Memorial Official Site
attractions.description: The National 9/11 Pentagon Memorial is a 2-acre outdoor space dedicated to honoring the 184 victims of the September 11, 2001 attack on the Pentagon. It features 184 cantilevered memorial benches, each with a small reflecting pool, arranged in a timeline based on the victims' ages. U.S. Department of War
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 Hours; Tuesday: Open 24 Hours; Wednesday: Open 24 Hours; Thursday: Open 24 Hours; Friday: Open 24 Hours; Saturday: Open 24 Hours; Sunday: Open 24 Hours Pentagon Memorial Official Site
attractions.admission: Free Washington.org
highlights: '184 Memorial Units (benches) with individual reflecting pools [Washington.org', 'An Age Wall that increases in height relative to the ages of the victims Washington.org', 'A Memorial Gateway marking the impact time of 9:37 a.m. Washington.org', 'Orientation of benches distinguishing those on Flight 77 from those in the building Washington.org', 'A landscape featuring Paperbark Maple trees and Crape Myrtles Washington.org']
attractions.tips: No public parking is available on-site; visitors should use the Metrorail to the Pentagon or Pentagon City stations. Photography is permitted inside the memorial but strictly prohibited elsewhere on the Pentagon Reservation. A free 24-minute audio tour is available by calling (202) 741-1004. StayArlington, Virginia.org
attractions.nearby: Arlington National Cemetery, Air Force Memorial, Pentagon City Mall AARP

attractions.name: Netherlands Carillon
attractions.description: The Netherlands Carillon is a 127-foot-tall open steel tower that stands as a symbol of friendship and gratitude from the people of the Netherlands to the United States for aid during and after World War II. It houses 53 bells and is situated in a peaceful park setting that offers some of the most iconic panoramic views of the Washington, D.C. skyline. (National Park Service, Netherlands and You)
attractions.hours: Grounds: Monday-Sunday, 6:00 AM - 12:00 AM. Automated chimes occur daily every 15 minutes from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM. (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: Free (National Park Service)
highlights: ['Houses 53 bells ranging from 37.5 pounds to over 12,000 pounds.', 'Provides spectacular views of the Lincoln Memorial, Washington Monument, and U.S. Capitol.', 'Features automated daily performances including national anthems and armed forces medleys.', 'Surrounded by thousands of tulips that bloom beautifully in the spring months.', 'Hosts live carillonneur concerts on select Sunday afternoons during the summer.']
attractions.tips: The grassy slope in front of the carillon is a premier spot for a sunset picnic with a view of the DC skyline. Visit in late March or April to see the vibrant tulip displays. Note that the tower itself is closed to the public for safety, so the bells must be enjoyed from the grounds. (National Park Service, Virginia.org)
attractions.nearby: U.S. Marine Corps War Memorial (Iwo Jima), Arlington National Cemetery, Arlington House (The Robert E. Lee Memorial) (National Park Service)

attractions.name: Old Town Alexandria (Alexandria Historic District)
attractions.description: Old Town is Alexandria’s beloved downtown and nationally designated historic district on the Potomac River waterfront. The King Street mile and surrounding brick-lined sidewalks are studded with centuries-old architecture, historic attractions, and independent businesses. It is a walkable neighborhood filled with restaurants, boutiques, galleries, and historic sites, anchored by the George Washington Masonic National Memorial to the west and the Potomac River to the east. (Visit Alexandria)
attractions.address: 221 King Street, Alexandria, VA 22314 (Alexandria Visitor Center)
attractions.hours: Visitor Center Hours (May-August): Monday-Sunday, 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; (September-April): Monday-Sunday, 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM. District shops and restaurants vary, typically opening around 10:00 AM and closing late evening. (Visit Alexandria)
attractions.admission: Free (Public Historic District); Attractions within Old Town vary; "Key to the City" museum pass available for $23. (Visit Alexandria)
highlights: 'Strolling the King Street mile, featuring over 200 independent restaurants and boutiques. ([Visit Alexandria)', "Exploring the Torpedo Factory Art Center, a former munitions factory turned into a hive of creativity with over 80 artists' studios. (Visit Alexandria)", 'Visiting the Old Town Farmers’ Market, the nation’s longest continually operating farmers’ market at the same site. (Visit Alexandria)', 'Taking a ride on the free King Street Trolley which connects the King St-Old Town Metro station to the waterfront. (Visit Alexandria)', 'Seeing historic landmarks like the Gadsby’s Tavern Museum and the Carlyle House. (Visit Alexandria)', 'Viewing the Spite House, the skinniest historic house in America at just seven feet wide. (Visit Alexandria)']
attractions.tips: Start your visit at the Alexandria Visitor Center in the historic Ramsay House for maps and tickets. Use the free King Street Trolley to save your feet, especially when traveling from the Metro to the waterfront. Wear comfortable walking shoes as many streets are original brick or cobblestone. For photographers, 'Captain’s Row' (100 block of Prince Street) offers iconic cobblestone views, especially during 'magic hour' before sunset. (Visit Alexandria, Helena Woods Guide)
attractions.nearby: George Washington's Mount Vernon, National Harbor, Washington D.C. monuments, and Jones Point Park. (Visit Alexandria)

attractions.name: United States Air Force Memorial
attractions.description: The United States Air Force Memorial honors the service and heritage of the men and women of the United States Air Force and its predecessor organizations. It features three soaring stainless steel spires designed by James Ingo Freed that reach up to 270 feet, symbolizing the spirit of flight and the Thunderbirds' signature maneuvers Official Website.
attractions.address: 1 Air Force Memorial Drive, Arlington, VA 22204
attractions.hours: Daily: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM (April 1 – Sept 30); 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM (Oct 1 – March 31). Closed on Christmas Day Official Website.
attractions.days_open: Open daily throughout the year, except for Christmas Day Official Website
attractions.admission: Free Official Website
highlights: ['Three soaring stainless steel spires, the tallest reaching 270 feet, representing jet contrails.', 'Bronze Honor Guard statue featuring four 8-foot-tall figures representing Air Force members.', "Two 56-foot-long granite inscription walls detailing the 'valor' and 'values' of the Air Force.", 'The Glass Contemplation Wall, a 10-foot tall structure honoring fallen airmen.', "A USAF 'star' symbol embedded in the granite beneath the spires.", 'Panoramic views of the Pentagon and the Washington, D.C. skyline.']
attractions.tips: Admission and parking are free, but parking is limited and available on a first-come, first-served basis. Due to ongoing construction, the sole entrance is currently via Columbia Pike. No food or smoking is permitted on the grounds, and only service animals are allowed Official Website.
attractions.nearby: Arlington National Cemetery, The Pentagon, National 9/11 Pentagon Memorial, Pentagon City Mall Free Tours by Foot

attractions.name: United States Marine Corps War Memorial (National Park Service)
attractions.description: The United States Marine Corps War Memorial is dedicated to all Marines who have given their lives in defense of the United States since 1775. The memorial features a massive bronze sculpture based on the iconic 1945 photograph of the flag-raising on Mount Suribachi during the Battle of Iwo Jima. It stands as a powerful symbol of the nation's gratitude and the valor of the Marine Corps. (National Park Service)
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM - 12:00 AM (Monday - Sunday) (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: Free (National Park Service)
highlights: 'Iconic 32-foot-tall bronze figures raising a 60-foot flagpole based on the famous Iwo Jima photograph ([National Park Service)', 'Polished Swedish black granite base inscribed with every major Marine Corps engagement since 1775 (Wikipedia)', 'One of the few official sites where the American flag flies 24 hours a day by presidential proclamation (National Park Service)', 'Spectacular panoramic views of the Lincoln Memorial, Washington Monument, and U.S. Capitol (National Park Service)', 'Summer Sunset Parades featuring the U.S. Marine Drum and Bugle Corps and the Silent Drill Platoon (National Park Service)']
attractions.tips: The memorial is best visited at sunrise or sunset for ideal photography lighting and clear views of the D.C. skyline. Limited free parking is available on-site, though it is prohibited during summer Tuesday evening Sunset Parades. Visitors are encouraged to use the Rosslyn or Arlington National Cemetery Metro stations, both of which are within a 15-minute walk. (National Park Service, Washington.org)
attractions.nearby: Netherlands Carillon, Arlington National Cemetery, Arlington House (The Robert E. Lee Memorial) (National Park Service)
